title: E-Commerce REST API
shortDescription: Scalable RESTful API powering a multi-tenant e-commerce platform with real-time inventory management.
context: Built for a startup that needed to support multiple vendor storefronts on a single platform. The system needed to handle high traffic during sales events while maintaining data consistency across tenants.
problem: The client's existing monolithic system couldn't scale during peak traffic periods, leading to slow response times and inventory sync issues between vendors. They needed a solution that could handle 10,000+ concurrent users.

solution.architecture: Implemented a clean MVC architecture with repository pattern for data access abstraction. Used service classes to encapsulate business logic, making the codebase testable and maintainable.
solution.authentication: JWT-based authentication with refresh token rotation. Implemented role-based access control (RBAC) with granular permissions for vendors, admins, and customers.
solution.database: Normalized MySQL schema with multi-tenant architecture using tenant_id foreign keys. Implemented database indexing strategies and query optimization for frequently accessed data.
endpoints: GET /api/v1/products - Paginated product listing with filters
POST /api/v1/orders - Create order with inventory validation
solution.errorHandling: Centralized exception handling with custom exception classes. All errors return consistent JSON responses with error codes, messages, and debug info in development mode.

title: Payment Gateway Integration
shortDescription: Secure payment processing service supporting multiple providers with automatic failover.
context: Developed for a fintech company that needed to process payments across multiple regions with different payment provider requirements. Required PCI-DSS compliance and 99.99% uptime.
problem: The company was losing transactions due to single provider failures and high processing fees in certain regions. They needed a unified API that could route payments intelligently.

solution.architecture: Microservice architecture with event-driven communication. Used the Strategy pattern for payment provider abstraction, allowing easy addition of new providers.
solution.authentication: API key authentication with request signing using HMAC-SHA256. Implemented rate limiting and IP whitelisting for additional security.
solution.database: PostgreSQL with encrypted columns for sensitive data. Implemented audit logging for all payment operations with immutable transaction history.
endpoints: POST /api/refunds - Process full or partial refund
solution.errorHandling: Circuit breaker pattern for provider failures with automatic failover. Retry logic with exponential backoff. Dead letter queue for failed webhooks.

title: Distributed Task Scheduler
shortDescription: High-availability job scheduling system processing millions of tasks daily with real-time monitoring.
context: Created for an enterprise client that needed to manage scheduled jobs across multiple services. The system needed to handle various job types from email campaigns to data processing.
problem: Existing cron-based solution was unreliable with no retry mechanism, no visibility into job status, and single point of failure. Jobs were being dropped during deployments.

solution.architecture: Distributed architecture with Redis as the message broker. Leader election for scheduler coordination. Worker pools for parallel job execution.
solution.authentication: Service-to-service authentication using mutual TLS. Admin dashboard uses OAuth 2.0 with PKCE flow.
solution.database: Redis for job queues and real-time state. PostgreSQL for job history and analytics. Time-series data in InfluxDB for metrics.
solution.errorHandling: Configurable retry policies per job type. Dead letter queue for permanently failed jobs. Alerting via webhooks and Slack integration.

title: Authentication Microservice
shortDescription: Centralized authentication service supporting SSO, MFA, and social login for enterprise applications.
context: Built as the cornerstone of a microservices ecosystem for a SaaS company. Needed to support both B2B (SSO with corporate identity providers) and B2C (social login) use cases.
problem: Each service was implementing its own authentication, leading to inconsistent security practices and poor user experience. Password reset and MFA were not standardized.

solution.architecture: Standalone service following OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect specifications. Implements authorization code flow, client credentials, and refresh token grant types.
solution.authentication: Password hashing with Argon2id. TOTP-based MFA with backup codes. WebAuthn support for passwordless authentication. Session management with Redis.
solution.database: PostgreSQL for user data with encrypted PII columns. Redis for session storage and rate limiting counters. Audit log with immutable entries.
solution.errorHandling: Standardized OAuth error responses. Account lockout after failed attempts. Security event logging for threat detection.
